788.reason: The grammar defines FuncRParams as optional ([FuncRParams]). The code unconditionally calls parseFuncRParams, which expects at least one Exp. When parsing func_p(), the next token is ')', causing parseFuncRParams to incorrectly attempt to parse an expression or produce incorrect output structure. It should check if curToken is RPARENT before calling parseFuncRParams.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 0.5 , reason:  [Error: When there are no parameters (e.g., func_p()), parseFuncRParams() is called but should not be, as FuncRParams is optional. This causes the parser to incorrectly consume the ')' token as part of an expression, leading to missing <UnaryExp> output before the ')' in the expected output.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 1.0 , reason:  The grammar states that FuncRParams is optional ([FuncRParams]). Calling parseFuncRParams unconditionally causes it to attempt parsing an expression when the next token is ')', leading to incorrect output structure (extra empty Exp nodes) and potentially consuming tokens incorrectly or failing to match the expected output format where empty parameter lists do not generate a <FuncRParams> node or inner expression nodes. It should check if curToken is RPARENT before calling.

849.reason: [Error: When the function call has no arguments (e.g., func_p()), parseFuncRParams should not be called, but here it is called unconditionally, causing an extra <FuncRParams> to be output. The grammar allows FuncRParams to be empty, but the parser does not handle the case where there are no parameters.]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 0.5 , reason:  [Error: This function always parses at least one Exp, but FuncRParams is optional (zero or more). When there are no parameters, it should not be called at all, as indicated in the UnaryExp rule.]

92.reason: Missing check for curPos >= length; accessing source.charAt(curPos++) will throw StringIndexOutOfBoundsException if curPos reaches the end of the source string.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 1.0 , reason:  Missing check for curPos >= length; accessing source.charAt(curPos++) will throw StringIndexOutOfBoundsException if curPos reaches the end of the source string.

962.code: for (int i = 0; look(i).getType() != LexType.SEMICN && look(i) != null; i += 1) {
962.reason: [The condition checks `look(i).getType()` before checking for null, which can cause a NullPointerException when `look(i)` returns null. Also, the loop may never terminate if a semicolon is missing, leading to an index out-of-bounds error.]
